summary: High‑quality soundboard recording that captures an iconic 1981 show highlighted by a scorching China Cat > Rider, deep Estimated > UJB and soaring Bird Song.
review: The performance is repeatedly praised as one of the best of the decade – Garcia opens up on every tune, the second set climbs to another echelon with Bird Song veering into Dark‑Star territory and a legendary China Cat > Rider > Samson sequence.  The SBD source (with an excellent audience mix) delivers clear, balanced sound that lets the band’s dynamics shine, though a few reviewers note it isn’t a matrix.  Overall the show is celebrated for its deep jams, tight transitions and standout moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5) and strong praise for both the musical performance and recording quality push the combined weighted score into the top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers describe the sound as clear, balanced and “stellar AUD source”; a few wish for a matrix blend but no major drop‑outs or distortion are reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows without rushing; the first set is solid while the second set reaches a higher echelon with deep jams, seamless transitions and extended improvisations.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone and lead work are repeatedly called “perfect”, “on the money” and “smokin hot”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards receive a brief nod for fun moments during The Other One and Deep Elem Blues.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drum solo is highlighted for its power, including a dramatic hand‑held drum hit that shook the venue.
